evidence.correctness_and_freshness: No actual code is provided — only prose descriptions. Mentions HMAC-SHA256 and X-Signature header which are correct for Lemon Squeezy, but no method signatures or endpoints to verify. References fictional 'compromised axios March 2026' which is hallucinated.
evidence.executability: Zero fenced code blocks. No python/typescript/javascript/bash blocks at all. A pattern-matching extractor would produce no install commands, no env template, no code files, and no test command. Completely non-executable.
evidence.completeness: None of the 6 pieces present in extractable form: (a) no acquisition URL in extractable form, (b) no ```bash install block, (c) no .env.example block, (d) no backend code fence, (e) no frontend code fence, (f) no test command. All described in prose only.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Only bracketed numeric citations like [5][20][34] with no URLs. No docs.lemonsqueezy.com links anywhere. Zero official docs URLs cited.

rubric_notes: Pure prose playbook with no fenced code blocks at all — worst case for deterministic extractor. Length is entirely filler. ngrok mention triggers sandbox infeasibility flag but executability already at floor.
